memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Ripley, Rippley, Rippley, Rippleigh, Ripleigh, Riplea and many more. First found in Yorkshire where they were seated from early times and their first records appeared on the census rolls taken by the ancient Kings of Britain to determine the rate of taxation of their subjects. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: William, Mary, Sarah and John Ripley who settled in Boston Mass. in 1638; William Ripley settled in Hingham Mass. in 1630; William Ripley settled in Carolina in 1774. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Recorded as Ripley and more rarely Riply and Riplee, this is an English medieval surname. It is locational and translates as 'The farm whose land cuts a strip through the forest' as the various places called Ripley in the counties of Derbyshire, Hampshire, Surrey and Yorkshire. However it is the village of Ripley in Yorkshire which seems to be originator of most surnames, and this place is in an area formerly known as the forest of Knaresbough, which would seem to confirm the meaning. The first recording of the the villages are in the Domesday Book of 1086 when Ripley in Derbyshire is recorded as 'Ripelie', and Ripley in Yorkshire as 'Ripelia'. The early surname development talen from surviving medieval rolls and charters includes Roger de Rippeley of Northumberland in 1242, whilst John de Ripley is given as being a freeman of the city of York in the year 1330, and Richard Rypplay in the Poll Tax rolls of the county of Yorkshire in 1379. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Bernard de Rippeley. This was dated 1175, in the early Charter Rolls of Yorkshire, during the reign of King Henry 2nd of England, 1154 - 1189. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was sometimes known as the Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Henry Rippon Ripley於1551年12月14日出生在英國越可爾諾萊安時，他的父親Cuthbert Ripon32歲，母親Eme Hoppey27歲。他於1570年2月9日與Margaret Walker在英國英格蘭越可爾爾斯同結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1635年在英國英格蘭越可爾吉爾達爾享年84歲，埋葬在英國越可爾爾斯同。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Henry Rippon Ripley于1551年12月14日出生在英国越可爾諾萊安时，他的父亲Cuthbert Ripon32岁，母亲Eme Hoppey27岁。他于1570年2月9日与Margaret Walker在英国英格兰越可爾爾斯同结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1635年在英国英格兰越可爾吉爾達爾享年84岁，埋葬在英国越可爾爾斯同。

nameMeanings.name: Ripley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Ripley (Derbyshire, Hampshire, Surrey, Yorkshire). The Hampshire and Surrey placenames derive from Old English ripel, rippel ‘strip of land’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. The Yorkshire placename probably derives from the Anglian tribe name Hrype + Old English lēah. The Derbyshire placename may derive from Old English ripel, rippel or the Anglian tribe name Hrype + Old English lēah.
History: William Ripley (died 1656) came from Wymondham, Norfolk, England, to Hingham, MA, in 1638.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: Rippon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Ripon in Yorkshire. The placename means ‘amongst the Hrype’, from the Anglian tribe name Hrype, dative plural Hrypum, of obscure etymology.

variants.name: Rippeon
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of English   Rippon  .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Henry Rippon Ripley was born on 14 December 1551, in Northowram, Yorkshire, England, his father, Cuthbert Ripon, was 32 and his mother, Eme Hoppey, was 27. He married Margaret Walker on 9 February 1570, in Rylstone, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1635, in Kildale, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 84, and was buried in Rylstone, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Henry Rippon Ripley est né le 14 décembre 1551, à Northowram, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Cuthbert Ripon, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Eme Hoppey, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Walker le 9 février 1570, à Rylstone, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1635, à Kildale, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterré à Rylstone, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Henry Rippon Ripley am 14. Dezember 1551 in Northowram, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Cuthbert Ripon 32 und seine Mutter Eme Hoppey 27. Er hat am 9. Februar 1570 in Rylstone, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Margaret Walker geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1635 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Kildale, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Rylstone, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Henry Rippon Ripley è nato il il 14 dicembre 1551 nel Northowram, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Cuthbert Ripon, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Eme Hoppey, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Walker il 9 febbraio 1570, nel Rylstone, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1635, nel Kildale, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolto a Rylstone, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Henry Rippon Ripleyが1551年12月14日にイギリスヨークシュアイアノーソウラムで生まれた時，父Cuthbert Riponは32歳，母Eme Hoppeyは27歳であった。1570年2月9日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアリルストーンでMargaret Walkerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1635年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアキルダルで84歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアリルストーンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Henry Rippon Ripley(이)가 1551년 12월 14일 영국 요르크실르 노르토램에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Cuthbert Ripon(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Eme Hoppey(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1570년 2월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 릴스톤에서 Margaret Walker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1635년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 킬데일에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 릴스톤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Henry Rippon Ripley nasceu em 14 de dezembro de 1551, em Northowram, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Cuthbert Ripon, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Eme Hoppey, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Walker em 9 de fevereiro de 1570, em Rylstone, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1635, em Kildale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 84 anos, e foi sepultado em Rylstone, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Henry Rippon Ripley родился 14 декабря 1551 г. в местности нортоурам, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Cuthbert Ripon было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Eme Hoppey, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Walker 9 февраля 1570 г. в местности рилстоун, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1635 г. в местности каилдеил, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 84 лет и был похоронен в местности рилстоун, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Henry Rippon Ripley nació el 14 de diciembre de 1551, en Northowram, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Cuthbert Ripon, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Eme Hoppey, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Margaret Walker el 9 de febrero de 1570, en Rylstone, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1635, en Kildale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultado en Rylstone, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
